_comment: Per-slug map: does a free .gov tool already cover this app fully? If covers_fully=true, the HULEC scorer flags the app as a retirement candidate on the Unique dimension. Operator confirms before retiring — this is a signal, not an auto-action. URL is the canonical .gov resource the user should be sent to instead.

foiahelper.note: FOIA.gov is request-routing only. It does not draft the request or appeal text. Our value is in the drafting + appeal walkthrough.

evictiondefense.note: LawHelp directs to local legal aid. It does not draft an Answer or stay motion.

wageclaimai.note: DOL intake form, not a wage-theft calculator + complaint drafter.

medicaidcheck.note: healthcare.gov screens federally but state Medicaid programs vary; our value is per-state appeal letters.

specialedai.note: IDEA site is statute reference, not IEP-letter drafter or dispute-resolution helper.

probatewalk.note: Probate is state-by-state; no single federal canonical.

overdrafthelp.note: CFPB has overdraft complaint guidance but no bank-fee-refund letter generator.

vinhistory.note: NHTSA decodes VINs but does not produce a buyer-readable history walkthrough.

smallclaimsus.note: USA.gov links to state courts but does not generate a complaint form.

unemploymentappeal.note: DOL has program info; states handle appeals individually and there's no federal letter generator.

section8nav.note: HUD Resource Locator is search-only; our app guides applications and waitlists.

nfirsassistant.note: USFA hosts NFIRS docs and the tool itself; our value is field-friendly walkthrough during incident reporting.

votingrightspro.note: Vote.gov is registration links only; our app handles rights-restoration in felony states.

voterregai.note: vote.gov fully covers basic voter registration. Operator should review whether VoterRegAI adds enough above vote.gov to keep alive.

vahousing.note: VA.gov has program info; our app handles HUD-VASH waitlists + landlord communication.

vaclaim.note: VA's eBenefits portal does not draft Notices of Disagreement or higher-level review submissions.

edusafeai.note: Title IX + Clery Act compliance has no single federal user-facing tool.

usalivingguide.note: USA.gov is a directory; our app's newcomer-onboarding flow is unique.

teachercerts.note: Teacher cert is state-administered; no federal canonical.

asylumprepai.note: USCIS form page is the form, not a country-conditions-aware prep walkthrough. Lexical refusal prevents form-fill, but case prep stays useful.

caphub.note: benefits.gov screens federally; our hub adds state CAP rules + appeal letters.

firstresponderhub.note: No single federal canonical for cross-discipline first-responder reference.

iep.note: IDEA hub is statute-level; no IEP drafting helper.
